revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name= Dake Rokusho Shrine (嶽六所神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Dakerokusyo_jinja.JPG|250px]] | location=[[Akita Prefecture|Akita]], [[Daisen City]], [[Jingūji (Daisen)|Jingūji]], Ochigai 7 | coordinates={{coord|39|28|50|N|140|26|11|E|region:JP05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Dake Rokusho Shrine}} | deity={{ill|Ōtano-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okutama-no-Mikoto|ja|興玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|句々土神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Amaterasu's Mirror|ja|磐戸命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|鹽槌翁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Classification={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (disputed), Former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year=Traditional date: Daibō period (701–704) | Festival=-- | Map={{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=28|lat_sec=50|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=11|label=Dake Rokusho Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|39|28|50|N|140|26|11|E}}}} }}
'''Dake Rokusho Shrine''' (嶽六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Akita Prefecture. It is a disputed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was historically classified as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.
It is a candidate for {{ill|Sohikahano Shrine|lt=Sohikahano Shrine|qid=Q135040055|1=Sohikahano Shrine}}. However as it is absent from the modern shrine database it is unclear whether the theory was discredited.<ref>https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182146</ref>
== History ==
[[File:Soegawa Jinja ato daisen.JPG|200px|thumb|right|Remains of Soegawa Shrine]] According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the Daibō period (701–704). It is identified as the location of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Soegawa Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Yamamoto District, One Shrine, Small, Soegawa Shrine."
During the medieval period, the shrine came under the influence of {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and became a site of {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} worship, causing the original deities to be forgotten. In the Edo period, {{ill|Satake Yoshikaku|ja|佐竹義格|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, lord of the Akita Domain, sought to restore ancient shrines, identifying the current {{ill|Soegawa Shrine|ja|副川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in present-day {{ill|Hachirōgata Town|ja|八郎潟町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Akita, as the Soegawa Shrine. However, research by scholars from {{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Shikinaisha Research Group, 'Shikinaisha Research Report Volume 12,' Kōgakkan University Press, 1981</ref> and local historians suggests that Dake Rokusho Shrine is the original Soegawa Shrine. Other theories, such as Shiga Tsuyoshi's<ref>Shiga Tsuyoshi, 'Shikinaisha Studies Volume 10: Tōsandō,' Yūzankaku, 1986, ISBN 4639006160</ref>, propose that {{ill|Soegawa Shinmeisha|ja|添川神明社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Akita City|ja|秋田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Akita, is the original site.
The associated Hachiman Shrine, the satellite shrine (里宮), became a Gō-sha in 1872 during the Meiji period.
== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ōtano-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Okutama-no-Mikoto|ja|興玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (both considered descendants of {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|句々土神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu's Mirror|ja|磐戸命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|鹽槌翁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== Festivals ==
The shrine hosts the {{ill|Bonten Matsuri|ja|梵天まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} every third Sunday of March.
== Satellite Shrine ==
[[File:Daisen akita hachiman jinja.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Hachiman Shrine]] '''Satellite Shrine (里宮): Hachiman Shrine''' (Daisen City, Jingūji, Jingūji 19-1) * Deities: {{ill|Hachiman|ja|保牟多和気命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|加句槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Traditional Founding: Daidō period (810 CE) * Note: Contains wooden plaques inscribed with the names of {{ill|Nakahara no Chikanaga|ja|中原親能|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyaji Kunihira|ja|宮道国平|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== Timeline == * Circa 700 CE (Daibō period): Traditional founding of Dake Rokusho Shrine. * Circa 810 CE (Daidō period): Traditional founding of Hachiman Shrine. * 1602 CE (Keichō 7): Satake Yoshinobu assumes control of Akita. * March 1868 (Meiji 1): Enactment of the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * May 14, 1871 (Meiji 4): Establishment of the shrine Ranking system. * 1872 CE (Meiji 5): Hachiman Shrine becomes a Gō-sha.
== Access == === By Bus === * Disembark at Atago-shita Bus Stop; 45-minute walk from {{ill|Jingūji Station|ja|神宮寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ōmagari Station (Akita)|ja|大曲駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ''' Operated by {{ill|Ugo Kōtsū|ja|羽後交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ''' {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Bonten Matsuri|ja|梵天まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Take Rokusho Shrine}}
